content.text: Lineage Metadata Standards for Claude Apps

Ad-hoc lineage schemas proliferate quickly when different teams instrument Claude differently. The compliance team cannot query across them; the data governance team cannot build tooling that works for every app. Adopting a shared metadata standard solves this: every Claude application emits the same field names, the same span types, and the same relationship model, making lineage queryable, comparable, and automatable at the enterprise level.

attrs.alt: Lineage metadata standards: W3C PROV to OpenTelemetry to Claude app field mapping

content.text: The diagram maps W3C PROV-DM's abstract concepts (Entity, Activity, Agent) onto OpenTelemetry spans, then to the concrete field names Claude applications should emit. This layered approach means your lineage data is compatible with both AI governance tooling (which speaks PROV) and standard observability infrastructure (which speaks OTEL).
a data deletion request requires finding every trace that used a given source document across all Claude apps, not just one.
a single lineage dashboard, anomaly detector, or compliance reporter works for every app that follows the standard.
regulators and auditors expect consistent record structures — non-standard schemas force manual reconciliation.
W3C PROV is an open standard; OTEL is CNCF-governed. Both are vendor-neutral and supported by multiple open-source stores.
The W3C PROV-DM Model Applied to Claude
W3C PROV-DM (Provenance Data Model) defines three first-class concepts and five core relations. Mapping Claude interactions onto this model gives your lineage records a shared vocabulary.
The following attribute names are the enterprise standard for Claude lineage spans. Every Claude application must populate these fields; optional fields should be included when applicable.
Unique identifier for the full user interaction
One of: user_input, system_prompt, rag_retrieval, tool_call, context_assembly, api_call, response
Span IDs this span depends on
Stable identifier for the Claude application (e.g. 'hr-assistant-v2')

# governance-extensions.yaml — append to every span that involves personal data
# These fields enable cross-app compliance queries

claude.data_classification:     # "public" | "internal" | "confidential" | "restricted"
claude.user_jurisdiction:       # "EU" | "UK" | "US" | "APAC" — origin of user data
claude.transfer_mechanism:      # "scc" | "adequacy" | "idta" | null
claude.tia_completed:           # true | false
claude.retention_class:         # "standard-90d" | "extended-2y" | "legal-hold"
claude.purpose_of_processing:   # GDPR Art. 13 purpose string, e.g. "customer-support"
claude.lawful_basis:            # "legitimate_interest" | "contract" | "consent" | ...
The following emitter class implements the canonical schema. Every Claude application imports and uses this class instead of writing custom logging.

import json, uuid, hashlib
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

LINEAGE_LOG = Path("logs/claude_lineage.jsonl")

@dataclass
class ClauseSpan:
    # Required for all spans
    claude_trace_id: str
    claude_span_id: str
    claude_span_type: str
    claude_parent_span_ids: list[str]
    claude_timestamp: str
    claude_app_id: str
    claude_environment: str
    # Additional fields collected per-span
    attributes: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def to_otel_dict(self) -> dict:
        """Serialise to an OTLP-compatible dictionary."""
        base = {
            "trace_id": self.claude_trace_id,
            "span_id": self.claude_span_id,
            "name": f"claude.{self.claude_span_type}",
            "parent_span_ids": self.claude_parent_span_ids,
            "start_time": self.claude_timestamp,
            "attributes": {
                "claude.span_type": self.claude_span_type,
                "claude.app_id": self.claude_app_id,
                "claude.environment": self.claude_environment,
                **{f"claude.{k}": v for k, v in self.attributes.items()},
            },
        }
        return base

class StandardLineageEmitter:
    def __init__(self, app_id: str, environment: str = "production"):
        self.app_id = app_id
        self.environment = environment

    def _new_span(self, trace_id: str, span_type: str,
                  parent_ids: list[str], attrs: dict) -> ClauseSpan:
        span = ClauseSpan(
            claude_trace_id=trace_id,
            claude_span_id=str(uuid.uuid4())[:8],
            claude_span_type=span_type,
            claude_parent_span_ids=parent_ids,
            claude_timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            claude_app_id=self.app_id,
            claude_environment=self.environment,
            attributes=attrs,
        )
        self._write(span)
        return span

    def _write(self, span: ClauseSpan):
        with LINEAGE_LOG.open("a") as f:
            f.write(json.dumps(span.to_otel_dict()) + "\n")

    def new_trace(self) -> str:
        return str(uuid.uuid4())

    def emit_user_input(self, trace_id: str, text: str, session_id: str,
                         classification: str = "internal",
                         jurisdiction: str = "US") -> ClauseSpan:
        return self._new_span(trace_id, "user_input", [], {
            "input_hash": hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest()[:16],
            "session_id": session_id,
            "data_classification": classification,
            "user_jurisdiction": jurisdiction,
        })

    def emit_rag_retrieval(self, trace_id: str, parent_id: str,
                            query: str, results: list[dict]) -> ClauseSpan:
        return self._new_span(trace_id, "rag_retrieval", [parent_id], {
            "rag.query_hash": hashlib.sha256(query.encode()).hexdigest()[:16],
            "rag.source_ids": [r["source_id"] for r in results],
            "rag.chunk_ids": [r["chunk_id"] for r in results],
            "rag.similarity_scores": [r.get("similarity_score", 0) for r in results],
        })

    def emit_api_call(self, trace_id: str, parent_id: str,
                       model: str, region: str, input_tokens: int,
                       request_id: str) -> ClauseSpan:
        return self._new_span(trace_id, "api_call", [parent_id], {
            "model": model,
            "region": region,
            "input_tokens": input_tokens,
            "request_id": request_id,
        })

    def emit_response(self, trace_id: str, parent_id: str,
                       output_text: str, output_tokens: int,
                       finish_reason: str, latency_ms: int) -> ClauseSpan:
        return self._new_span(trace_id, "response", [parent_id], {
            "output_hash": hashlib.sha256(output_text.encode()).hexdigest()[:16],
            "output_tokens": output_tokens,
            "finish_reason": finish_reason,
            "latency_ms": latency_ms,
        })
Each application that emits lineage must be validated before deployment. A standards compliance checker reads sampled spans and verifies required fields are present and correctly typed.
Most enterprise data catalogs (Apache Atlas, DataHub, Collibra) can ingest lineage via their APIs. The standard span format can be transformed into each catalog's native lineage model.

Lineage metadata standards for Claude applications align every team on the same field names, span types, and relationship model — making enterprise-wide governance queries possible. The standard maps W3C PROV-DM concepts (Entity, Activity, Agent) onto OpenTelemetry spans with Claude-specific attribute names prefixed claude.*. Required fields covering trace identity, span type, model, region, and content hashes must appear on every span; governance extension fields (jurisdiction, transfer mechanism, TIA status) enable compliance queries across apps. Implement a shared StandardLineageEmitter class that all Claude applications import, and validate compliance with an automated span auditor before deployment. Lineage data in this format can be pushed to enterprise data catalogs such as Apache Atlas or DataHub to provide cross-system lineage visibility.
